channel.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Edgar's Personal Wiki

item.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
item.description: Edgar’s Personal Wiki Public notebook This is the public edge of a private working wiki: source-backed notes about software, systems, books, institutions, and the moments where fiction explains reality better than reality explains itself.

item.title: cipher-and-intelligence-in-quicksilver
item.description: Cipher and Intelligence in Quicksilver Book Two of Quicksilver broadens intelligence from formal cryptography into a whole ecology of signals: vagabond gossip, courier routes, coffee-house messages, market prices, diplomatic staging, and Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz’s cipher teaching.

item.title: eliza-commercial-intelligence
item.description: Eliza’s Commercial Intelligence Eliza’s Book Two power in Quicksilver is commercial intelligence: the ability to read how goods, money, social roles, letters, and rumors can be converted into leverage.

item.title: jack-shaftoes-paris-hallucination
item.description: Jack Shaftoe’s Paris Hallucination Jack Shaftoe’s Paris hallucination closes a major Book Two movement in Quicksilver by turning physical collapse, theological argument, class humiliation, and quicksilver imagery into one grotesque street pageant.

item.title: quicksilver-commerce-and-money
item.description: Quicksilver, Commerce, and Money In Quicksilver, quicksilver is both a material substance and a governing metaphor.

item.title: slavery-and-punishment-in-quicksilver
item.description: Slavery and Punishment in Quicksilver Book Two of Quicksilver treats slavery and punishment as infrastructure, not backdrop.

item.title: vagabondage-in-quicksilver
item.description: Vagabondage in Quicksilver In Quicksilver, vagabondage is not only poverty or criminality.

item.title: eliza
item.description: Eliza Fictional Qwghlmian-born former slave, trader, cipher correspondent, and political operator in Neal Stephenson’s Quicksilver.

item.title: enoch-root
item.description: Enoch Root Recurring Neal Stephenson character appearing in both Quicksilver and Cryptonomicon.

item.title: gomer-bolstrood
item.description: Gomer Bolstrood Protestant political actor in Quicksilver, introduced into Eliza’s Book Two Hague network.
